page.content: The data on ownership types reveals that, under the header "Type of ownership," the national sample percentages and corresponding number of health facilities to sample are as follows: for "Public facilities," the national sample is 62% with 121 health facilities; "Private not-for-profit" accounts for 18% with 35 health facilities; "Private for-profit" comprises 17% with 33 health facilities; and "HIV research clinics" represent 3% with 6 health facilities, resulting in a total of 100% and 195 health facilities sampled.

cells.cell_text: Take some and leave the rest
Take all drugs even when symptoms disappear
Do not complete dose due to financial constraint

page.content: In a separate analysis of drug adherence patterns, the first table lists patterns with corresponding data for students (n=60) and villagers (n=60): 17 students, or 28.3%, stop drugs when symptoms disappear, while 24 villagers, or 40.0%, do the same. Eight students (13.3%) stop when tired of the drug, compared to 11 villagers (18.3%). Ten students (16.7%) take some and leave the rest, while 8 villagers (13.3%) follow this pattern. Additionally, 25 students (41.7%) take all drugs even when symptoms disappear, versus 10 villagers (16.7%). Lastly, no students (0.0%) do not complete the dose due to financial constraint, while 7 villagers (11.7%) report this issue.
